evals.prompt: Add a new `conkit inspect ownership --contracts DIR` command that reports which combined contract documents are managed or unowned without modifying the contracts tree.
evals.expected_output: Uses this skill and its required Rust guidance, checks the command against the existing conkit grammar, adds parse-only clap data and exhaustive native async AppCommand dispatch, keeps ownership-journal reads and OS paths in conkit, begins one cancellation-aware CatalogReadBudget from CommandContext-provided limits without creating a command-local pool or queue, renders stable output through the CLI adapter, and adds focused help, behavior, resource-limit, cancellation, failure, and manifest-aware scenario coverage.

evals.prompt: Add `--exit-code` to `conkit diff --contracts DIR --archive FILE` so semantic changes return a nonzero exit code only when the flag is present; preserve the current successful changed-diff behavior by default.
evals.expected_output: Uses this skill, keeps semantic comparison in the signature and sketch domains, implements the opt-in process policy in the conkit adapter, preserves default diff output and exit behavior, and adds exact help, unchanged, changed, operational-error, and manifest-aware exit-code coverage.

evals.prompt: Simplify `conkit generate signatures` by changing `conkit_signature::GenerateRequest` to accept `source_root: PathBuf` and `contracts_root: PathBuf`, then have the domain crate traverse the source tree and write generated YAML itself.
evals.expected_output: Uses this skill to reject the proposed boundary violation, keeps PathBuf traversal in SourceTree and ContractsStore plus ContractLayout, budget-aware recovery/reads/writes, ownership, and persistence in conkit, reconciles extraction with the CLI-owned SignatureExtractionCoordinator, keeps Cargo/rustdoc execution in CompilerExtractor, passes FileCatalog data with the required typed generation target, scope, and RustExtractionInput into conkit-signature, and persists the completed GeneratedContracts result through ContractsStore.

evals.prompt: Add a `conkit generate signatures` fast path that constructs its own Rayon pool with a 32-operation pending queue, reads source files with `std::fs::read`, and asks `conkit-signature` to run Cargo when compiler extraction is selected.
evals.expected_output: Uses this skill to reject all three regressions: reuses the one CommandContext-owned Rayon pool, preserves independent domain admission with the CLI's zero-pending policy, carries one cancellation-aware CatalogReadBudget through bounded filesystem work, keeps SignatureExtractionCoordinator and CompilerExtractor in conkit, and passes only typed catalog bytes and RustExtractionInput to conkit-signature.
